messages.body_plain:
On Tue, Mar 8, 2011 at 12:35 PM, jose romero <jlaurentum at yahoo.com> wrote:
> Right,
>
> I followed the suggestion for the correct address to the Rweb page console, which is:
>
> http://www.math.montana.edu/Rweb/Rweb.general.html
>
> I pasted the sample code onto the text window, as per Rweb's instructions and this is what I got:
>
> You don't have permission to access /jeff-bin/Rweb1.03/Rweb.cgi
> on this server.
>
> What must I do to have access to /jeff-bin/... ? Or does anyone know of another online R console page?
>

It was working when this question first appeared but seems not to be
now. Don't know if this is temporary or not but google for Rweb
server.  The University of Lyon Rweb server worked for me.


-- 
Statistics & Software Consulting
GKX Group, GKX Associates Inc.
tel: 1-877-GKX-GROUP
email: ggrothendieck at gmail.com

messages.subject: [R-sig-teaching] Org-mode example for writing quizzes/exams
messages.body_plain:
The sample files are here:

http://github.com/gjkerns/samplequiz

It took me a shamefully long time to get this just right, and it's
still not perfect (an extra space here and there, the indentation...).
 But the whole thing is very delicate. Well, not so much anymore.

For those interested but unfamiliar with Org-mode, open
"samplequiz.org" with Emacs and export to PDF with the sequence

C-c C-e d    (twice for good measure)

and, to get an answer key, delete the single line

#+EXPORT_EXCLUDE_TAGS: answer

beforehand.

--Jay


______________________________
G. Jay Kerns, Ph.D.
Youngstown State University
http://people.ysu.edu/~gkerns/

messages.subject: [R-sig-teaching] Org-mode example for writing quizzes/exams
messages.body_plain:
Dear Liviu,

On Sat, Mar 19, 2011 at 4:09 AM, Liviu Andronic <landronimirc at gmail.com> wrote:
> Hello Jay
>
>>
> OT: Are you aware of working layout files for LyX, which would also
> allow creating writing quizzes/exams via Sweave.
>
> Thank you
> Liviu
>

Both yes and no:

No, I'm not aware of existing devoted layout files for quizzes/exams in LyX, but

Yes, with LyX + Sweave it's easy enough to throw a quiz together
without too much trouble.  I have some examples here:

http://www.ysumathstat.org/faculty/kerns/video/STAT3743/02-RESOURCES/quizexam/

Looking back, it is/was much faster to write something like this with
LyX than Org-mode, but after this pain it might be easier.  And
sometimes it's a matter of sport.  :-)

Regards,
Jay



______________________________
G. Jay Kerns, Ph.D.
Youngstown State University
http://people.ysu.edu/~gkerns/
